content_en:
628 Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations

Abu Hurairah 4 reported that the Prophet 2% said, “Whoever believes in Allah and His Messenger, establishes the prayer, and fasts Ramadan, then it is a right upon Allah to admit him into Paradise, regardless of whether he migrated in the way of Allah or sat in the land that he was born in.” They said, “O Messenger of Allah, will you not inform the people?” He said, “In Paradise there are 100 degrees (stations, rankings), which Allah prepared from those who fought in His Way. Between each degree and the next is as the distance between the sky and the earth. Then when you ask Allah, ask Him for Al-Firdaus, for it is the middle of Paradise and the highest part of Paradise, and above it is the Throne of the Most Merciful, and from it the rivers of Paradise are made to gush forth ~ or gush forth (Abu ‘Aaamir, one of the narrators, is the one who doubted).” (Ahmad) And Bukhaaree related a hadith through a different chain and with a similar meaning.

Al-Firdaus Is The Highest Level In Paradise; Prayer And Fasting Necessitate The Forgiveness Of Allah ‘Azza Wa-Jall (To Him belongs Might and Majesty)

Mu’aadh Ibn Jabal # related that he heard the Messenger of Allah #8 say, “Whoever prays these five prayers and fasts Ramadan — (the narrator says,) ‘I do not know whether he mentioned the Zakaat or not?’ — then it is a right upon Allah to forgive him, regardless of whether he migrated or whether he sat (ie. stayed) where his mother gave him birth.” Mu’aadh - said, “I said, ‘O Messenger of Allah, should I not go out and inform the people?” He 2 said, “No, leave the people to work, for there are 100 levels in Paradise, and the distance between one level and the next is like the distance between the heavens and the earth. And the highest of those levels is the Firdaus, and over it is the Throne. It (Firdaus) is the most middle part of Paradise, and from it the rivers of Paradise gush forth. So when you ask Allah, ask Him for the Firdaus.” (Related by Abul-Qaasim At-Tabaraanee) At-Tirmidhee related the same, but through a different chain of narrators. And Tbn Maajah also related it with another chain, but his narration is a shortened version.

It Is From Al-Firdaus That The Rivers Of Paradise Gush Forth

“‘Ubaadah ibn As-Saamit 4 related that the Prophet 2% said, “Paradise is 100 levels; between each level and the next is the

Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations 629.

distance of 100 years.” Ibn ‘Affaan said, “Like the distance between the heavens and the earth, and the Firdaus is the highest of them in. degree, and from it the four rivers (of Paradise) emerge. And the Throne is above it. So when you ask Allah, ask Him for Al-Firdaus.” (Ahmad) At-Tirmidhee related it through a different chain of narrators.

The said description is only suitable for something that is domeshaped, for the highest part of a dome is also the most middle part of it, And Allah $@ knows best.

The Levels Of Paradise Differ, And None Knows The Degree Of Those Differences Except For Allah, The Lord Of All That Exists

Abu Hurairah 4 reported that the Messenger of Allah #8 said, “Paradise is 100 levels; the distance between one of those levels and the one that follows it is equal to the distance of 500 years.” (Related by Abu Bakr ibn Abee Daawood) At-Tirmidhee related it through a different chain with the wording, “The distance between one level and the next is 100 years.” He then said, “This hadith is Hasan sahih,””

Abu Sa’eed 4: related that the Messenger of Allah #€ said, “Paradise is 100 levels; were all that exists to gather in one of them, it would be spacious enough for them.” (Related by Al-Haafiz Abu Ya‘laa) AtTirmidhee and Ahmad related it as well.

A Mention Of What The Dweller Of Paradise With The Lowest Ranking Will Receive And What The One With The Highest Ranking Will Receive In Terms Of A Vast And Huge Territory

Allah 8% said: al ae “And when you look there (in Paradise), you will see a delight (that

cannot be imagined), and a great dominion’

630 Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations

As for the last person to enter Paradise from the Prophet's nation, we have already related the narration of ‘IIgimah ibn Mas‘ood from the Prophet #%, in which is the following: “Are you not pleased that for you is the equivalent of the world and ten times more?” (Bukhaaree and Muslim)

The following is a hadith that Ibn ‘Umar .& ascribed to the Prophet 8%: “Verily, the dweller of Paradise who has the lowest ranking therein is one who will look at his gardens, his bliss, and his servants from the distance of 1000 years. And the most honorable of them (of the dwellers of Paradise) with Allah is one who will look at His Countenance (the Countenance of Allah) in the beginning of the day and at the end of the day.” He 2% then recited this Verse:

4M G0 bab as

In another narration, Ibn ‘Umar 4 reported that the Messenger of Allah #8 said, “The dweller of Paradise who is least in ranking will look at a territory that is 2000 years (in distance). He will see its furthest point just as he sees its nearest point. He will see his wives and his servants. And the best of them (dwellers of Paradise) in ranking is one who will look at the countenance of Allah twice every day.” (Ahmad) At-Tirmidhee related it through a different chain.

The following narration is related from Al-Mugheerah ibn Sho’bah a through ‘Abdul-Malik ibn Sa’eed ibn Abjar and Mutarraf (Ibn Abjar said that it is a saying of the Prophet 2% whereas Mutarraf did not): “Moosa said, ‘O my Lord, inform me of the dweller of Paradise who is the lowest in ranking.’ He said, ‘Yes, he is a man who will come after the people have settled in their abodes and after they have taken what was for them to take. It will be said to him: enter Paradise. Then he will say, ‘O my Lord, how can I enter it when the people have settled in their abodes and have taken what is for them to take?’ He will say to him, ‘Does it not please you that you should have the equivalent of what a king from the kings of the earth had?” He will say, ‘lam pleased, O my Lord.’ He will say, ‘For you is what is equivalent and what is equivalent (a second time {ie., twice as

Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations 631

much)).’ (Sufyaan, one of the narrators, intertwined his five fingers). He will say, ‘I am pleased, O my Lord.’ Then Moosa said, ‘O my Lord, then inform me of the dweller of Paradise who is highest in ranking.’ He said, ‘Yes, they are the ones I wanted (to tell you about), and I will inform you about them. I planted their honor (Le., the honor and reward they will receive) with My Hands, and then 1 stamped a seal over it. Then no eye has seen (it), no ear has heard (of it), and it has not been thought of (or fathomed) by the heart of a human being.” (Related by Muslim and At-Tabaraanee, and the wording here is that of At-Tabaraanee)

And the confirmation of that is in the Book of Allah &:

chee th epee AR Ae oath ee see ge 65h BE GTS gl Gh GEG SH Os Sep

“No person knows what is kept hidden for them of joy as a reward

for what they used to do.’"! Abu Hurairah 2 reported that the Prophet #¥ said, “Allah ‘Azza WaJall (To Him belongs Might and Majesty) said, ‘I have prepared for My righteous slaves that which no eye has seen, which no ear has heard, and which has not occurred to the heart of any human being.” (Related by Bukhaaree and Muslim, and the wording here is that of Muslim)

The confirmation of this is in Allah’s Book: here AG i gee PR A Sh rath EG OK OTe 46S YEG ie gl ot fh GEG OE LS Sap “No person knows what is kept hidden for them of joy as a reward for what they used to do," Sahl ibn Sa’ad a said, “I attended a gathering of the Messenger of Allah 2% in which he #% described Paradise, until he finished. At the end of his hadith (talk), he #% said, ‘In it is that which no eye has seen, which no ear has heard, and which has not occurred to the heart of any human being.” He #f then recited this Verse:

5 US OE RE GAS Go SB SMD

we A A a th ie oe TE Bod eneice & gis ot AGG CHE KO fae

632 Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations

“Their sides forsake their beds, to invoke their Lord in fear and hope, and they spend (in charity in Allah’s 88 cause) out of what We have besiowed on them. No person knows what is kept hidden for them of joy as a reward for what they used to do." (Ahmad)

A Mention Of The Rooms Of Paradise, Their Elevation, Vastness, And Hugeness; We Ask Allah From His Grace To Grant It To Us From The Great Abundance Of His Favors

CF gt Se Oe ah ot Se a Bt atl Sb o ¥ tee Bog ee

And Allah # said:

itu Bt 9 UE wal &H A aclp

“As for such, there will be twofold reward for what they did, and they will reside in the high dwellings (Paradise) in peace and security’) Abu Sa’eed 2 reported that the Messenger of Allah 2 said, “Verily, the dwellers of Paradise will look from afar at the insides of the rooms of those above them just as they see — or you see — a glistening star that is going away in the horizon, from the east or the west, due to the disparity between them.” They said, “O Messenger

Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations 633
content_id:
628 Kitab Akhir Zaman: Fitnah dan Cobaan Besar

Abu Hurairah رضي الله عنه melaporkan bahwa Nabi صلى الله عليه وسلم bersabda, "Barang siapa beriman kepada Allah dan Rasul-Nya, mendirikan shalat, dan berpuasa di bulan Ramadan, maka adalah hak bagi Allah untuk memasukkannya ke dalam Surga, terlepas dari apakah dia berhijrah di jalan Allah atau tinggal di negeri tempat dia dilahirkan." Mereka berkata, "Wahai Rasulullah, apakah engkau tidak akan memberitahu orang-orang?" Beliau bersabda, "Di Surga ada 100 derajat (kedudukan, peringkat), yang Allah persiapkan untuk mereka yang berjuang di jalan-Nya. Antara setiap derajat dan derajat berikutnya adalah seperti jarak antara langit dan bumi. Maka ketika kalian memohon kepada Allah, mohonlah kepada-Nya Al-Firdaus, karena itu adalah bagian tengah Surga dan bagian tertinggi Surga, dan di atasnya adalah Arsy Yang Maha Pengasih, dan dari sanalah sungai-sungai Surga mengalir deras — atau mengalir deras (Abu 'Aamir, salah satu perawi, adalah orang yang ragu)." (Ahmad) Dan Bukhari meriwayatkan sebuah hadis melalui rantai yang berbeda dengan makna yang serupa.

Al-Firdaus Adalah Tingkatan Tertinggi di Surga; Shalat dan Puasa Mengharuskan Ampunan Allah 'Azza Wa-Jall (Bagi-Nya Kemuliaan dan Keagungan)

Mu'aadz ibn Jabal رضي الله عنه meriwayatkan bahwa dia mendengar Rasulullah صلى الله عليه وسلم bersabda, "Barang siapa mengerjakan shalat lima waktu ini dan berpuasa di bulan Ramadan — (perawi berkata,) 'Aku tidak tahu apakah dia menyebut Zakat atau tidak?' — maka adalah hak bagi Allah untuk mengampuninya, terlepas dari apakah dia berhijrah atau tinggal (yaitu menetap) di tempat ibunya melahirkannya." Mu'aadz رضي الله عنه berkata, "Aku berkata, 'Wahai Rasulullah, haruskah aku keluar dan memberitahu orang-orang?' Beliau صلى الله عليه وسلم bersabda, 'Tidak, biarkan orang-orang beramal, karena di Surga ada 100 tingkatan, dan jarak antara satu tingkatan dan tingkatan berikutnya seperti jarak antara langit dan bumi. Dan yang tertinggi dari tingkatan-tingkatan itu adalah Al-Firdaus, dan di atasnya adalah Arsy. Itu (Al-Firdaus) adalah bagian paling tengah dari Surga, dan dari sanalah sungai-sungai Surga mengalir deras. Maka ketika kalian memohon kepada Allah, mohonlah kepada-Nya Al-Firdaus.'" (Diriwayatkan oleh Abul-Qaasim At-Thabaraanee) At-Tirmidzi meriwayatkan hal yang sama, tetapi melalui rantai perawi yang berbeda. Dan Ibnu Maajah juga meriwayatkannya dengan rantai lain, tetapi narasinya adalah versi yang dipersingkat.

Dari Al-Firdaus Itulah Sungai-Sungai Surga Mengalir Deras

'Ubaadah ibn As-Saamit رضي الله عنه meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi صلى الله عليه وسلم bersabda, "Surga memiliki 100 tingkatan; antara setiap tingkatan dan tingkatan berikutnya adalah

Kitab Akhir Zaman: Fitnah dan Cobaan Besar 629

jarak 100 tahun." Ibnu 'Affaan berkata, "Seperti jarak antara langit dan bumi, dan Al-Firdaus adalah yang tertinggi di antara mereka dalam derajat, dan dari sanalah empat sungai (Surga) muncul. Dan Arsy berada di atasnya. Maka ketika kalian memohon kepada Allah, mohonlah kepada-Nya Al-Firdaus." (Ahmad) At-Tirmidzi meriwayatkannya melalui rantai perawi yang berbeda.

Gambaran yang disebutkan hanya cocok untuk sesuatu yang berbentuk kubah, karena bagian tertinggi dari kubah juga merupakan bagian paling tengahnya. Dan Allah عز وجل lebih mengetahui.

Tingkatan-Tingkatan Surga Berbeda-Beda, dan Tidak Ada yang Mengetahui Derajat Perbedaan-Perbedaan Itu Kecuali Allah, Tuhan Semesta Alam

Abu Hurairah رضي الله عنه melaporkan bahwa Rasulullah صلى الله عليه وسلم bersabda, "Surga memiliki 100 tingkatan; jarak antara satu tingkatan dan tingkatan yang mengikutinya sama dengan jarak 500 tahun." (Diriwayatkan oleh Abu Bakr ibn Abee Daawood) At-Tirmidzi meriwayatkannya melalui rantai yang berbeda dengan lafaz, "Jarak antara satu tingkatan dan tingkatan berikutnya adalah 100 tahun." Kemudian dia berkata, "Hadis ini Hasan sahih."

Abu Sa'eed رضي الله عنه meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah صلى الله عليه وسلم bersabda, "Surga memiliki 100 tingkatan; seandainya semua yang ada berkumpul di salah satunya, niscaya akan cukup luas untuk mereka." (Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Haafiz Abu Ya'laa) At-Tirmidzi dan Ahmad juga meriwayatkannya.

Penyebutan tentang Apa yang Akan Diterima oleh Penghuni Surga dengan Peringkat Terendah dan Apa yang Akan Diterima oleh Penghuni dengan Peringkat Tertinggi dalam Hal Wilayah yang Luas dan Besar

Allah عز وجل berfirman: "Dan ketika kamu melihat ke sana (di Surga), kamu akan melihat kenikmatan (yang tidak bisa dibayangkan), dan kerajaan yang besar."

630 Kitab Akhir Zaman: Fitnah dan Cobaan Besar

Adapun orang terakhir yang masuk Surga dari umat Nabi صلى الله عليه وسلم, kami telah meriwayatkan narasi 'Alqamah ibn Mas'ood dari Nabi صلى الله عليه وسلم, yang di dalamnya terdapat: "Tidakkah kamu senang bahwa untukmu adalah yang setara dengan dunia dan sepuluh kali lebih banyak?" (Bukhari dan Muslim)

Berikut ini adalah hadis yang Ibnu 'Umar رضي الله عنه sandarkan kepada Nabi صلى الله عليه وسلم: "Sesungguhnya, penghuni Surga yang memiliki peringkat terendah di dalamnya adalah orang yang akan melihat taman-tamannya, kenikmatan-kenikmatannya, dan para pelayannya dari jarak 1000 tahun. Dan yang paling mulia di antara mereka (penghuni Surga) di sisi Allah adalah orang yang akan melihat Wajah-Nya (Wajah Allah) di awal hari dan di penghujung hari." Beliau صلى الله عليه وسلم kemudian membacakan ayat ini:

Dalam riwayat lain, Ibnu 'Umar رضي الله عنه melaporkan bahwa Rasulullah صلى الله عليه وسلم bersabda, "Penghuni Surga yang berperingkat paling rendah akan melihat wilayah seluas 2000 tahun (jaraknya). Dia akan melihat ujung terjauhnya seperti dia melihat ujung terdekatnya. Dia akan melihat istri-istrinya dan para pelayannya. Dan yang terbaik di antara mereka (penghuni Surga) dalam peringkat adalah orang yang akan melihat wajah Allah dua kali setiap hari." (Ahmad) At-Tirmidzi meriwayatkannya melalui rantai yang berbeda.

Narasi berikut diriwayatkan dari Al-Mugheerah ibn Syu'bah رضي الله عنه melalui 'Abdul-Malik ibn Sa'eed ibn Abjar dan Mutharraf (Ibnu Abjar mengatakan bahwa itu adalah sabda Nabi صلى الله عليه وسلم sedangkan Mutharraf tidak): "Musa berkata, 'Wahai Tuhanku, beritahukan kepadaku tentang penghuni Surga yang paling rendah peringkatnya.' Dia berfirman, 'Ya, dia adalah seorang pria yang akan datang setelah orang-orang telah menetap di kediaman mereka dan setelah mereka mengambil apa yang untuk mereka ambil. Akan dikatakan kepadanya: masuklah ke Surga. Kemudian dia akan berkata, 'Wahai Tuhanku, bagaimana aku bisa memasukinya sedangkan orang-orang telah menetap di kediaman mereka dan telah mengambil apa yang untuk mereka?' Dia akan berfirman kepadanya, 'Apakah tidak menyenangkanmu jika kamu memiliki yang setara dengan apa yang dimiliki seorang raja dari raja-raja di bumi?' Dia akan berkata, 'Aku ridha, wahai Tuhanku.' Dia akan berfirman, 'Untukmu adalah yang setara dan yang setara (untuk kedua kalinya (yaitu dua kali

Kitab Akhir Zaman: Fitnah dan Cobaan Besar 631

lipat)).' (Sufyaan, salah satu perawi, menjalin kelima jarinya). Dia akan berkata, 'Aku ridha, wahai Tuhanku.' Kemudian Musa berkata, 'Wahai Tuhanku, beritahukan kepadaku tentang penghuni Surga yang memiliki peringkat tertinggi.' Dia berfirman, 'Ya, mereka adalah orang-orang yang Aku kehendaki (untuk memberitahumu), dan Aku akan memberitahumu tentang mereka. Aku menanamkan kehormatan mereka (yaitu kehormatan dan pahala yang akan mereka terima) dengan Tangan-Ku, kemudian Aku menstempel segel di atasnya. Kemudian tidak ada mata yang pernah melihatnya, tidak ada telinga yang pernah mendengarnya, dan itu tidak pernah terlintas di hati seorang manusia pun.'" (Diriwayatkan oleh Muslim dan At-Thabaraanee, dan lafaz di sini adalah milik At-Thabaraanee)

Dan konfirmasi dari itu ada dalam Kitab Allah عز وجل:

"Tidak ada jiwa yang mengetahui apa yang disembunyikan untuk mereka berupa kesenangan sebagai balasan atas apa yang mereka kerjakan."

Abu Hurairah رضي الله عنه melaporkan bahwa Nabi صلى الله عليه وسلم bersabda, "Allah 'Azza Wa-Jall (Bagi-Nya Kemuliaan dan Keagungan) berfirman, 'Aku telah menyiapkan untuk hamba-hamba-Ku yang saleh sesuatu yang tidak pernah dilihat oleh mata, yang tidak pernah didengar oleh telinga, dan yang tidak pernah terlintas di hati seorang manusia pun.'" (Diriwayatkan oleh Bukhari dan Muslim, dan lafaz di sini adalah milik Muslim)

Konfirmasi dari ini ada dalam Kitab Allah عز وجل: "Tidak ada jiwa yang mengetahui apa yang disembunyikan untuk mereka berupa kesenangan sebagai balasan atas apa yang mereka kerjakan." Sahl ibn Sa'ad رضي الله عنه berkata, "Aku menghadiri sebuah majelis Rasulullah صلى الله عليه وسلم di mana beliau صلى الله عليه وسلم menggambarkan Surga, hingga beliau selesai. Di akhir hadisnya (pembicaraannya), beliau صلى الله عليه وسلم bersabda, 'Di dalamnya ada sesuatu yang tidak pernah dilihat oleh mata, yang tidak pernah didengar oleh telinga, dan yang tidak pernah terlintas di hati seorang manusia pun.'" Kemudian beliau صلى الله عليه وسلم membacakan ayat ini:

632 Kitab Akhir Zaman: Fitnah dan Cobaan Besar

"Lambung mereka jauh dari tempat tidurnya, mereka berdoa kepada Tuhannya dengan rasa takut dan harapan, dan mereka menginfakkan (dalam amal kebajikan di jalan Allah عز وجل) dari apa yang Kami anugerahkan kepada mereka. Tidak ada jiwa yang mengetahui apa yang disembunyikan untuk mereka berupa kesenangan sebagai balasan atas apa yang mereka kerjakan." (Ahmad)

Penyebutan tentang Kamar-Kamar Surga, Ketinggian, Keluasan, dan Kebesarannya; Kami Memohon kepada Allah dari Karunia-Nya untuk Menganugerahkannya kepada Kami dari Kelimpahan Nikmat-Nya yang Besar

Dan Allah عز وجل berfirman:

"Adapun mereka itu, akan ada dua kali lipat pahala atas apa yang mereka kerjakan, dan mereka akan tinggal di tempat-tempat yang tinggi (Surga) dalam keadaan aman dan tenteram." Abu Sa'eed رضي الله عنه melaporkan bahwa Rasulullah صلى الله عليه وسلم bersabda, "Sesungguhnya, penghuni Surga akan melihat dari jauh bagian dalam kamar-kamar mereka yang ada di atas seperti mereka melihat — atau kalian melihat — bintang yang berkilauan yang menjauh di cakrawala, dari timur atau barat, karena perbedaan di antara mereka." Mereka berkata, "Wahai Rasulullah

Kitab Akhir Zaman: Fitnah dan Cobaan Besar 633
content_ar: 628 الكتاب of The End: Great الابتلاءات and Tribulations أبو هريرة 4 reported that النبي ﷺ 2% said, “Whoever believes in الله and His Messenger, establishes the الصلاة, and fasts Ramadan, then it is a الحق upon الله to admit him into الجنة, regardless of whether he migrated in the الطريق of الله or sat in the land that he was born in.” قالوا, “O Messenger of الله, will you not inform the people?” قال, “In الجنة there are 100 degrees (stations, rankings), which الله prepared from those who fought in His الطريق. Between each degree and the next is as the distance between the sky and the earth. Then عندما you ask الله, ask Him for Al-Firdaus, for it is the middle of الجنة and the highest part of الجنة, and above it is the Throne of the Most Merciful, and from it the rivers of الجنة are made to gush forth ~ or gush forth (Abu ‘Aaamir, one of the narrators, is the one who doubted).” (أحمد) And Bukhaaree related a الحديث through a different chain and with a similar meaning. Al-Firdaus Is The Highest Level In الجنة; الصلاة And الصيام Necessitate The المغفرة Of الله ‘Azza Wa-Jall (To Him belongs Might and Majesty) Mu’aadh Ibn Jabal # related that he heard الرسول ﷺ of الله #8 say, “Whoever prays these five الصلوات and fasts Ramadan — (the narrator says,) ‘I do not know whether ذكر the Zakaat or not?’ — then it is a الحق upon الله to forgive him, regardless of whether he migrated or whether he sat (ie. stayed) where his الأم gave him birth.” Mu’aadh - said, “I said, ‘O Messenger of الله, should I not go out and inform the people?” He 2 said, “No, leave the people to work, for there are 100 levels in الجنة, and the distance between one level and the next is like the distance between the heavens and the earth. And the highest of those levels is the Firdaus, and over it is the Throne. It (Firdaus) is the most middle part of الجنة, and from it the rivers of الجنة gush forth. لذا عندما you ask الله, ask Him for the Firdaus.” (Related by Abul-Qaasim At-Tabaraanee) At-Tirmidhee related the same, but through a different chain of narrators. And Tbn Maajah أيضاً related it with another chain, but his narration is a shortened version. It Is From Al-Firdaus That The Rivers Of الجنة Gush Forth “‘Ubaadah ibn As-Saamit 4 related that النبي ﷺ 2% said, “الجنة is 100 levels; between each level and the next is the الكتاب of The End: Great الابتلاءات and Tribulations 629. distance of 100 years.” Ibn ‘Affaan said, “Like the distance between the heavens and the earth, and the Firdaus is the highest of them in. degree, and from it the four rivers (of الجنة) emerge. And the Throne is above it. لذا عندما you ask الله, ask Him for Al-Firdaus.” (أحمد) At-Tirmidhee related it through a different chain of narrators. Tقال description is only suitable for something that is domeshaped, for the highest part of a dome is أيضاً the most middle part of it, And الله $@ knows best. The Levels Of الجنة Differ, And None Knows The Degree Of Those Differences Except For الله, The Lord Of All That Exists أبو هريرة 4 reported that الرسول ﷺ of الله #8 said, “الجنة is 100 levels; the distance between one of those levels and the one that follows it is equal to the distance of 500 years.” (Related by أبو بكر ibn Abee Daawood) At-Tirmidhee related it through a different chain with the wording, “The distance between one level and the next is 100 years.” He then said, “This الحديث is حسن صحيح,”” Abu Sa’eed 4: related that الرسول ﷺ of الله #€ said, “الجنة is 100 levels; were all that exists to gather in one of them, it would be spacious enough for them.” (Related by Al-Haafiz Abu Ya‘laa) AtTirmidhee and أحمد related it as well. A Mention Of What The Dweller Of الجنة With The Lowest Ranking Will Receive And What The One With The Highest Ranking Will Receive In Terms Of A Vast And Huge Territory الله 8% said: al ae “And عندما you look there (in الجنة), you will see a delight (that cannot be imagined), and a great dominion’ 630 الكتاب of The End: Great الابتلاءات and Tribulations أما the last person to enter الجنة from النبي ﷺ's nation, we have already related the narration of ‘IIgimah ibn Mas‘ood from النبي ﷺ #%, in which is the following: “Are you not pleased that for you is the equivalent of the الدنيا and ten times more?” (Bukhaaree and مسلم) The following is a الحديث that Ibn ‘عمر .& ascribed to النبي ﷺ 8%: “Verily, the dweller of الجنة who has the lowest ranking therein is one who will look at his gardens, his bliss, and his servants from the distance of 1000 years. And the most honorable of them (of the dwellers of الجنة) with الله is one who will look at His Countenance (the Countenance of الله) in the beginning of the day and at the end of the day.” He 2% then recited this الآية: 4M G0 bab as In another narration, Ibn ‘عمر 4 reported that الرسول ﷺ of الله #8 said, “The dweller of الجنة who is least in ranking will look at a territory that is 2000 years (in distance). He will see its furthest point just as he sees its nearest point. He will see his wives and his servants. And the best of them (dwellers of الجنة) in ranking is one who will look at the countenance of الله twice every day.” (أحمد) At-Tirmidhee related it through a different chain. The following narration is related from Al-Mugheerah ibn Sho’bah a through ‘Abdul-مالك ibn Sa’eed ibn Abjar and Mutarraf (Ibn Abjar said that it is a saying of النبي ﷺ 2% في حين Mutarraf did not): “Moosa said, ‘O my Lord, inform me of the dweller of الجنة who is the lowest in ranking.’ قال, ‘Yes, he is a man who will come بعد the people have settled in their abodes and بعد they have taken what wأما them to take. It will be said to him: enter الجنة. Then he will say, ‘O my Lord, how can I enter it عندما the people have settled in their abodes and have taken what is for them to take?’ He will say to him, ‘Does it not please you that you should have the equivalent of what a king from the kings of the earth had?” He will say, ‘lam pleased, O my Lord.’ He will say, ‘For you is what is equivalent and what is equivalent (a ثانياً time {ie., twice as الكتاب of The End: Great الابتلاءات and Tribulations 631 much)).’ (Sufyaan, one of the narrators, intertwined his five fingers). He will say, ‘I am pleased, O my Lord.’ Then Moosa said, ‘O my Lord, then inform me of the dweller of الجنة who is highest in ranking.’ قال, ‘Yes, they are the ones I wanted (to tell you about), and I will inform you about them. I planted their الشرف (Le., the الشرف and الأجر they will receive) with My Hands, and then 1 stamped a seal over it. Then no eye has seen (it), no ear has heard (of it), and it has not been thought of (or fathomed) by the القلب of a human being.” (Related by مسلم and At-Tabaraanee, and the wording here is that of At-Tabaraanee) And the confirmation of that is in the الكتاب of الله &: chee th epee AR Ae oath ee see ge 65h BE GTS gl Gh GEG SH Os Sep “No person knows what is kept hidden for them of joy as a الأجر for what they used to do.’"! أبو هريرة 2 reported that النبي ﷺ #¥ said, “الله ‘Azza WaJall (To Him belongs Might and Majesty) said, ‘I have prepared for My الصالح slaves that which no eye has seen, which no ear has heard, and which has not occurred to the القلب of any human being.” (Related by Bukhaaree and مسلم, and the wording here is that of مسلم) The confirmation of this is in الله’s الكتاب: here AG i gee PR A Sh rath EG OK OTe 46S YEG ie gl ot fh GEG OE LS Sap “No person knows what is kept hidden for them of joy as a الأجر for what they used to do," Sahl ibn Sa’ad a said, “I attended a gathering of الرسول ﷺ of الله 2% in which he #% described الجنة, حتى he finished. At the end of his الحديث (talk), he #% said, ‘In it is that which no eye has seen, which no ear has heard, and which has not occurred to the القلب of any human being.” He #f then recited this الآية: 5 US OE RE GAS Go SB SMD we A A a th ie oe TE Bod eneice & gis ot AGG CHE KO fae 632 الكتاب of The End: Great الابتلاءات and Tribulations “Their sides forsake their beds, to invoke their Lord in الخوف and الأمل, and they spend (in الصدقة in الله’s 88 cause) out of what We have besiowed on them. No person knows what is kept hidden for them of joy as a الأجر for what they used to do." (أحمد) A Mention Of The Rooms Of الجنة, Their Elevation, Vastness, And Hugeness; We Ask الله From His الفضل To Grant It To Us From The Great Abundance Of His Favors CF gt Se Oe ah ot Se a Bt atl Sb o ¥ tee Bog ee And الله # said: itu Bt 9 UE wal &H A aclp “أما such, there will be twofold الأجر for what they did, and they will reside in the high dwellings (الجنة) in peace and security’) Abu Sa’eed 2 reported that الرسول ﷺ of الله 2 said, “Verily, the dwellers of الجنة will look from afar at the insides of the rooms of those above them just as they see — or you see — a glistening star that is going away in the horizon, from the east or the west, بسبب the disparity between them.” قالوا, “O Messenger الكتاب of The End: Great الابتلاءات and Tribulations 633
